summary: Added US-842 integration coverage for all-pass, misere, contract-phase progression, third-card trick resolution, next-lead/trick-count mutation, and rendered legal-action projection using the existing Vitest/jsdom stack across domain applyAction/getLegalActions and UI renderGame action identities.

tests_run.result: failed before fix, then passed after test correction
tests_run.evidence: Initial red run exposed five mismatches between the first draft expectations and the current human-control/render boundaries plus full-trick sequencing; final focused run passed 5 integration tests.
Vitest passed 11 files / 99 tests and the production build completed successfully with tsc and vite build.

node_package_scripts.verify: npm run test && npm run build

verification_environment.notes: No backend or live API applies; integration evidence comes from local project integration tests spanning domain state transitions and jsdom UI rendering.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Fixed-profile all-pass, misere, whist, and ten-game behavior in integrated flows
evidence_matrix.expected: All-pass progresses with progressive-state carryover, misere skips whist exposure, whistable contracts expose pass/whist/half-whist, and ten-level whist decisions expose check-only behavior.
evidence_matrix.evidence: src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts covers all-pass bidding->play->settlement->next-deal, misere bidding->play->settlement, contract whist decisions with half-whist/pass, and representative ten-level render projection for check-only controls.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Required phase and transition coverage through the full rule flow
evidence_matrix.expected: Integration tests cover bidding, contract ordering, widow pickup, discard, whist decision, play, settlement, and next-deal continuation using legal actions only.
evidence_matrix.evidence: src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts drives all-pass, misere, and contract paths entirely through getLegalActions/applyAction and checks representative renderGame action projections for human states.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Third-card trick resolution updates winner, trick counts, and next lead
evidence_matrix.expected: The third legal card clears the trick, increments the winning seat's trick count, and assigns the next actor to the winner or settlement actor on the final trick.
evidence_matrix.evidence: src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts resolves a trump-winning third card through applyAction after asserting the single legal play from getLegalActions, then checks cleared trick, trick count mutation, and winner actor.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Rendered human legal-action exposure stays inside rule-engine legality
evidence_matrix.expected: Rendered controls are a subset or projection of getLegalActions for representative bidding, contract order, whist, play, settlement, and next-deal states.
evidence_matrix.evidence: src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts compares renderGame action identities and enabled card controls against getLegalActions-derived keys, including discard projection handling and ten-game check-only exposure.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Completed trick mutation is visible at integration level
evidence_matrix.expected: A completed three-card trick mutates state by clearing currentTrick, incrementing the correct trick counter, and promoting the winner to lead or settlement actor.
evidence_matrix.evidence: src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts asserts post-trick state after the third-card action in both flowing scenarios and the dedicated trick-resolution test.
